title: The Gang Gets a New Member

jokes.text: Charlie's 'butt, butt, butt...Open up the time capsule' song with dancing
jokes.setup: Opening a time capsule should be a meaningful moment
jokes.punchline: Charlie turns it into a nonsensical butt song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie behavior - turning something meaningful into childish absurdity. High character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: butt, butt, butt...Open up the time capsule

jokes.text: I could watch him do that for hours
jokes.punchline: Mac's genuine appreciation for the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment showing Mac's low standards for entertainment

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I could watch him do that for hours

jokes.text: Gregg Jeffries baseball card - 'Future All-Star, future All-Star' / 'It wasn't valuable then'
jokes.setup: Items they thought would be valuable
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing 'future All-Star' for a card that wasn't even valuable at the time
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about bad investments and misplaced confidence

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Gregg Jeffries baseball card - 'Future All-Star, future All-Star'

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It wasn't valuable then

jokes.text: Dee's letter to future self about Hollywood success with enclosed million dollar check
jokes.punchline: Absurdly delusional assumptions about guaranteed success
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dee's delusional narcissism and sets up her arc

dialogue.line: Dee's letter to future self about Hollywood success with enclosed million dollar check

jokes.text: It's the fattening
jokes.setup: Dee getting worked up about hormones
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact comment about her weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank line - economical, dark, and delivered with characteristic bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's the fattening

jokes.text: Who's this jag-off?
jokes.punchline: Frank's blunt assessment of Schmidty
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - no filter, cutting through the nostalgia

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Who's this jag-off?

jokes.text: You pushed him out of a moving car and yelled 'You're out of the gang!'
jokes.setup: Trying to remember why they ditched Schmidty
jokes.punchline: Revealing the absurdly extreme method of ejection
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - casual mention of extreme violence as routine gang business

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You pushed him out of a moving car and yelled 'You're out of the gang!'

jokes.text: Now, in hindsight, that does seem a little rash
jokes.setup: Pushing someone out of a moving car
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement calling it 'a little rash'
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement - treating attempted murder as a minor social faux pas

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now, in hindsight, that does seem a little rash

jokes.text: Mrs. Schmidt says he's dead, then Schmidty jumps down stairs yelling 'What's up bitches?'
jokes.punchline: His dramatic entrance immediately after
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with physical comedy and perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Schmidt
dialogue.line: he's dead

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: What's up bitches?

jokes.text: He made me say it / I made you say it, listen to you, mom
jokes.setup: Wondering why his mom said he was dead
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of manipulating his disabled mother
jokes.explanation: Establishes Schmidty as equally terrible as the gang

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: He made me say it

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Schmidt
dialogue.line: I made you say it, listen to you, mom

jokes.text: Not that little, you know? Regular size. Maybe he, but... Not anymore.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's awkward insistence that he's regular size now
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie insecurity about his physical stature

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Not that little, you know? Regular size. Maybe he, but... Not anymore.

jokes.text: Charlie's weight argument - 'I tacked on mass, muscle mass'
jokes.setup: Schmidty pointing out Charlie gained weight
jokes.punchline: Charlie's delusional insistence it's muscle
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie delusion with Mac backing him up despite obvious truth

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I tacked on mass, muscle mass

jokes.text: Who's this old son of a bitch? / Don't kiss him
jokes.setup: Meeting Frank for the first time
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostility and Mac's protective warning
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment and Mac's bizarre specific warning

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Who's this old son of a bitch?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Don't kiss him

jokes.text: Get on your knees and ask / Show some humility
jokes.punchline: Demanding literal prostration
jokes.explanation: Good power play showing Schmidty's manipulative nature

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: Get on your knees and ask

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: Show some humility

jokes.text: I'm kidding / Come on, guys, have some dignity
jokes.setup: Making them get on their knees
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was a test while shaming them
jokes.explanation: Good reversal that establishes his superiority over them

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: I'm kidding

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: Come on, guys, have some dignity

jokes.text: I was drinking a lot back then, so if I did anything inappropriate...
jokes.punchline: His immediate assumption he may have been a creep
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about teacher-student relationships and alcoholism

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Myers
dialogue.line: I was drinking a lot back then, so if I did anything inappropriate...

jokes.text: May you rot in hell, you retched beast! / That's what the villagers screamed at you
jokes.punchline: It's actually a line from the play she was in
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on Dee's insecurities

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: May you rot in hell, you retched beast! That's what the villagers screamed at you

jokes.text: You were such a hideous delight in that show.
jokes.setup: A teacher complimenting a former student
jokes.punchline: 'Hideous delight' is a backhanded compliment about playing a monster
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward compliment that could be taken as an insult

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Myers
dialogue.line: You were such a hideous delight in that show.

jokes.text: You were such a hideous delight in that show
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about her hideous appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect backhanded compliment that hits Dee's insecurity

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Myers
dialogue.line: You were such a hideous delight in that show

jokes.text: No my dear, you must give up. You must. It's far too late for you
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about her lack of talent
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - the opposite of what an inspiring teacher should say

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Myers
dialogue.line: No my dear, you must give up. You must. It's far too late for you

jokes.text: The countdown pressure into accepting the teaching job
jokes.punchline: High-pressure countdown forcing her into it
jokes.explanation: Decent manipulation tactic but not particularly strong

dialogue.line: The countdown pressure into accepting the teaching job

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I mean, it's the MS that's really lame
jokes.setup: Being called lame for living with his mom
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that she has MS
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about serious illness delivered casually, makes the gang look bad

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I mean, it's the MS that's really lame

jokes.text: Kind of a downer, that story, though
jokes.setup: Heartfelt story about caring for dying mother
jokes.punchline: Mac's tone-deaf response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac - completely missing emotional cues and making it about himself

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Kind of a downer, that story, though

jokes.text: I am. I'm his living situation / Yeah, we share a bed
jokes.punchline: Frank describes himself as Mac's 'living situation'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Frank logic and phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I am. I'm his living situation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, we share a bed

jokes.text: Night Crawlers discussion and blanket strategy
jokes.punchline: Detailed discussion of imaginary game rules
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Charlie found someone who gets his weird games

dialogue.line: Night Crawlers discussion and blanket strategy

jokes.text: You take a piece of dirt. Exactly, you can use it as a force field, or as a roaming base.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain the mysterious Night Crawlers game
jokes.punchline: The 'explanation' makes no sense and involves dirt as force fields
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdity - the more they explain Night Crawlers, the less sense it makes

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You take a piece of dirt.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Exactly, you can use it as a force field, or as a roaming base.

jokes.text: Charlie's embarrassing butt dance performance
jokes.setup: Trying to recreate his earlier success
jokes.punchline: Awkward, forced performance that falls flat
jokes.explanation: Shows how forced repetition kills comedy

dialogue.line: Charlie's embarrassing butt dance performance

jokes.text: It is either him, or it is me. Him. Yeah. I was going to say him, too.
jokes.setup: Charlie gives them an ultimatum between him and Schmidty
jokes.punchline: They immediately and unanimously choose Schmidty without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Brutal and immediate rejection - no hesitation, discussion, or consideration for Charlie

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It is either him, or it is me.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Him.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. I was going to say him, too.

jokes.text: It is either him, or it is me / Him / Yeah / I was going to say him, too
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately choosing Schmidty without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Charlie's plan backfires spectacularly with unanimous rejection

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It is either him, or it is me

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Him

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I was going to say him, too

jokes.text: You-you may not have seen all of them or any of them.
jokes.setup: Student asks what Dee has acted in
jokes.punchline: Her defensive admission that they probably haven't seen any of her work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee insecurity - admits her lack of success while trying to seem successful

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You-you may not have seen all of them or any of them.

jokes.text: Yeah, said the turkey
jokes.punchline: Calling her a turkey to her face
jokes.explanation: Direct hit on Dee's bird-like appearance, economical and brutal

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Yeah, said the turkey

jokes.text: You can, uh, read, pull out your phones, whatever. They do love their phones.
jokes.setup: Students are misbehaving during Dee's presentation
jokes.punchline: Dr. Myers completely gives up and lets them ignore the lesson
jokes.explanation: Shows Dr. Myers' complete surrender to student apathy

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Myers
dialogue.line: You can, uh, read, pull out your phones, whatever. They do love their phones.

jokes.text: My enthusiasm in those days was fueled entirely by the alcohol
jokes.setup: Dee asking where his inspiration went
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he was drunk all the time
jokes.explanation: Dark and economical - explains his fall from grace perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Myers
dialogue.line: My enthusiasm in those days was fueled entirely by the alcohol

jokes.text: What's going on? You guys are gonna try and bang me afterwards or something?
jokes.punchline: Assuming they want to have sex with him
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but funny assumption about their intentions

dialogue.line: What's going on? You guys are gonna try and bang me afterwards or something?

jokes.text: Sterling silver clover ring ceremony
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate ceremony with cheap rings
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist ceremony that shows how seriously they take themselves

dialogue.line: Sterling silver clover ring ceremony

jokes.text: Why don't you just order what you want? Uh, well, Dennis kind of has the best palate. Yeah, I have the most refined palate, Schmidty.
jokes.setup: Schmidty questions why Dennis orders for everyone
jokes.punchline: Their pathetic justification that Dennis has the 'most refined palate'
jokes.explanation: Shows their weird group dynamics and Dennis's narcissistic control

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: Why don't you just order what you want?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Uh, well, Dennis kind of has the best palate.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, I have the most refined palate, Schmidty.

jokes.text: Well, it's 'cause it's not a shrimp, Frank; it's a prawn. / Yeah, and I don't like prawns, so I eat around them.
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pretentious correction and Frank's illogical response
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing Dennis's pretension and Frank's weird logic.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, it's 'cause it's not a shrimp, Frank; it's a prawn.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I don't like prawns, so I eat around them.

jokes.text: I hate salad / You hate salad? Where is this coming from?
jokes.punchline: Mac finally asserting basic preference and Dennis's shock
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing how controlled their relationship is

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I hate salad

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You hate salad? Where is this coming from?

jokes.text: Dennis, I hate salad. You hate salad? Where is this coming from?
jokes.punchline: Mac admits he hates salad, shocking Dennis
jokes.explanation: Shows how Schmidty's presence makes them question their dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dennis, I hate salad.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You hate salad? Where is this coming from?

jokes.text: Charlie's Bruce Jenner photo as résumé
jokes.punchline: Using random celebrity photo to represent excellence
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist Charlie logic - using a photo to represent abstract concepts

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Bruce Jenner photo as résumé

jokes.text: Charlie offering to swallow eraser whole to prove dedication
jokes.setup: Trying to prove his passion for janitorial work
jokes.punchline: Extreme gesture of eating office supplies
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie escalation - taking dedication to bizarre extremes

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'll swallow this eraser whole to prove my dedication to janitorial work.

jokes.text: Well, I'm asking you if it's edible because it certainly smells of grapes.
jokes.setup: Principal says the eraser isn't edible
jokes.punchline: Charlie thinks grape scent means it's edible
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie logic - scent equals edibility in his mind

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, I'm asking you if it's edible because it certainly smells of grapes.

jokes.text: What's crappenin', fellas?
jokes.punchline: Silly portmanteau of 'crap' and 'happening'
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay but fits Schmidty's try-hard personality

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: What's crappenin', fellas?

jokes.text: Schmidty not wearing the ring and being late
jokes.punchline: Complete disregard for their rules
jokes.explanation: Shows Schmidty doesn't respect their elaborate rules

dialogue.line: Schmidty not wearing the ring and being late

jokes.text: Yeah, the Mafia, the Freemasons, the White House. / The White House, they do it.
jokes.punchline: Comparing themselves to serious organizations, including the White House
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comparison elevating their stupid ritual.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, the Mafia, the Freemasons, the White House.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The White House, they do it.

jokes.text: Mac's failed ceremonial roundhouse kick to the bell
jokes.punchline: Complete whiff and pathetic attempt
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac martial arts delusion meeting reality

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Mac's failed ceremonial roundhouse kick to the bell

jokes.text: Uh-oh, whiff! I just missed that. Dude, you're not even close.
jokes.setup: Mac attempts his ceremonial roundhouse kick
jokes.punchline: He completely misses the bell
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy undermining Mac's attempt to look cool

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Uh-oh, whiff! I just missed that.

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: Dude, you're not even close.

jokes.text: What are you doing, trying to push me out of a moving car again?
jokes.punchline: He realizes they're repeating their past pattern
jokes.explanation: Great callback - shows they're literally repeating their past terrible behavior

dialogue.speaker: Schmidty
dialogue.line: What are you doing, trying to push me out of a moving car again?

jokes.text: Give me back the ring, all right? 'Cause you don't deserve it! / Get out of here! You're out of the gang, Schmidty!
jokes.punchline: Taking back the stupid ring they just gave him
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the ring ceremony and shows how quickly they turn.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Give me back the ring, all right? 'Cause you don't deserve it!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Get out of here! You're out of the gang, Schmidty!

jokes.text: Rise and shine, dickwad
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate way to address a teacher
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dee taking charge inappropriately

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Rise and shine, dickwad

jokes.text: Rise and shine, dickwad. This is a great teacher, but he is wasted on you.
jokes.setup: Dee is trying to inspire the students
jokes.punchline: She insults them while claiming the teacher is great
jokes.explanation: Dee's backwards motivational technique - insulting students to inspire them

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Rise and shine, dickwad. This is a great teacher, but he is wasted on you.

jokes.text: Students leaving when given the option
jokes.setup: Dee saying anyone can leave who doesn't want to be there
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately leaving
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective showing of Dee's failure

jokes.text: You get out of here, you stupid idiot. Hey, you want to go to the mall?
jokes.setup: Dee kicks out a student for being disruptive
jokes.punchline: Student casually asks another to go to the mall, showing no impact
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's complete lack of authority with students

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You get out of here, you stupid idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Hey, you want to go to the mall?

jokes.text: ♪ My face composed of rotten flesh, roar, roar! A hideous beast with deadly clutch, roar, roar! Another's heart beats in my chest I'll never know a woman's touch... Roar! ♪
jokes.setup: Dee performs Frankenstein for the students
jokes.punchline: Her dramatically over-the-top monster performance
jokes.explanation: Cringe performance showing Dee's desperate attempt to recapture past glory

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: ♪ My face composed of rotten flesh, roar, roar! A hideous beast with deadly clutch, roar, roar! Another's heart beats in my chest I'll never know a woman's touch... Roar! ♪

jokes.text: Oh, whoops. That's not... I am shattered to pieces! No, I'm not acting, you bitch! I think I've broke my hip.
jokes.punchline: He slips and actually gets injured, breaking character to insult Dee
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from theatrical to real injury, with the teacher insulting Dee

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Myers
dialogue.line: Oh, whoops. That's not... I am shattered to pieces! No, I'm not acting, you bitch! I think I've broke my hip.

jokes.text: And, uh, we pushed him out of a moving car for you.
jokes.setup: They're apologizing to Charlie and want him back
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions they pushed Schmidty from a car
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing they've made violence a casual part of their friendship

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: And, uh, we pushed him out of a moving car for you.

jokes.text: Charlie's content with being a janitor and refusing to rejoin the gang
jokes.setup: Gang trying to get Charlie back after rejecting him
jokes.punchline: Charlie finding genuine satisfaction in janitorial work
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending - Charlie actually better off without them

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm content with being a janitor and refusing to rejoin the gang
